title: Nannothelypteris nervosa
taxonomy: Nannothelypteris nervosa is classified within the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns commonly known as the marsh ferns or the strap ferns. This classification places Nannothelypteris nervosa in the order Polypodiales, a division of ferns that also includes the families Dryopteridaceae and Woodsiaceae. The genus Nannothelypteris is further classified within the subfamily Thelypteridoideae, a group of ferns that are mostly terrestrial and have strap-like fronds. The species Nannothelypteris nervosa is native to tropical and subtropical regions, where it can be found growing in damp environments. The classification of Nannothelypteris nervosa reflects its unique characteristics and growth habits as a member of the Thelypteridaceae family.

intro: Nannothelypteris nervosa, also known as the narrow-leaved shield fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing and the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma.
